perturbation.original_text: (p) "MBE Center" shall mean each franchise of MBE operating a franchise ---------- retail outlet within the United States which enters into a Subscription Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: (p) "MBE Center" shall mean each franchise of MBE operating a company owned store within the United States which enters into a Subscription Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'MBE Center' is changed to refer to 'company owned store' rather than 'franchise retail outlet'. This introduces an ambiguity because the original text throughout the agreement refers to 'franchise' and 'franchisee' model. This contradicts the rest of the document, which operates under the assumption that MBE Centers are franchises, not company-owned stores. This contradiction creates uncertainty about the scope of the agreement. Perturbation being made at Section 1(p).
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Service. The Company will operate the Service at its network operating ------- Source: STAMPS.COM INC, 10-Q, 11/14/2000 center. The Company shall bear all costs associated with the network operating center, including without limitation hardware and software, networking equipment and bandwidth charges, redundant storage and/or mirroring across multiple geographic locations, and maintenance; provided, however, that Company and MBE -------- ------- shall each pay one-half of the costs and expenses associated with transmitting information and data from the servers maintained or used by the Company to the VSAT network hub, including hardware and backhaul circuits. The Company will make the Service available to MBE Centers via one or more secure Internet sites. Each MBE Center approved by MBE that wishes to participate in the Service shall enter into a Subscription Agreement with the Company in a form to be mutually agreed upon by the parties, which form -4- shall include appropriate obligations of the MBE Centers as set forth in this Agreement (the "Subscription Agreement"). MBE agrees to use its commercially ---------------------- reasonable best efforts to facilitate the execution of a Subscription Agreement by each participating MBE Center within a reasonable amount of time after the Effective Date. The Company hereby grants the MBE Centers a [***]* under all of --- Company's Intellectual Property Rights solely to access and use the Service in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement and the Subscription Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'MBE Center' from a 'franchise retail outlet' to a 'company owned store'. The contradicted text explicitly discusses the relationship between 'The Company' and 'MBE Centers' in the context of a franchise model, including obligations for 'MBE' to facilitate agreements with participating MBE Centers. It also mentions 'franchisee'. By changing the definition, the original text is now directly contradicted because it assumes the existence of franchisees, which the new definition undermines. This change introduces a clear and direct contradiction because the contradicted text discusses the franchise model. The Subscription Agreement is referred to in the context of 'MBE Centers', meaning there is now an inconsistency in the document.

perturbation.original_text: (x) "Service Documentation" shall mean all manuals, instructions or other --------------------- Source: STAMPS.COM INC, 10-Q, 11/14/2000 information provided by the Company to MBE or MBE Centers which directly relate to the functionality and operation of the Service.
perturbation.changed_text: (x) "Service Documentation" shall mean all communications and marketing materials provided by the Company to MBE or MBE Centers related in any way to the Service.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Service Documentation' is altered to be broad marketing materials instead of technical manuals and operational instructions. This shift contradicts the sections that rely on the documentation being technical in nature. Perturbation made at Section 1(x).
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Limited Access. MBE and MBE Centers agree not to provide or otherwise -------------- make available any Service Documentation or other Confidential Information of the Company to any person other than employees, consultants, contractors or agents of MBE and MBE Centers with a ---------- need to use such Service Documentation or Confidential Information in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Service Documentation' focused on technical manuals and operational instructions, whereas the changed definition expands it to include all communications and marketing materials. The contradicted text limits access to 'Service Documentation' to employees who need it for the agreement's terms. If 'Service Documentation' now includes marketing materials, the restriction on access becomes less meaningful and potentially contradictory. It's difficult to argue that all marketing materials require the same level of restricted access as technical documents, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (aa) "Update" shall mean any and all bug fixes, error corrections, and ------ maintenance updates of the Service.
perturbation.changed_text: (aa) "Update" shall mean any optional feature enhancements that may be added to the service.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Update' is changed to mean optional feature enhancements rather than critical bug fixes and maintenance. This introduces a contradiction with section 4(a), which expects Updates to include essential service revisions. This creates a discrepancy on what 'Update' means, in section 4(a) refers to mandatory updates but with the changes being made in section 1(aa), now Updates are optional.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Updates and Service Revisions. The Company will make Updates to the ----------------------------- Service in accordance with the requirements set forth in the Specifications at no charge for the entire term of this Agreement. Without limiting the foregoing, the Service shall be updated from time to time during the term of this Agreement to include accurate and current (i) Listings and (ii) pricing and service descriptions for each carrier supported by the Manifest, in each case as soon as reasonably practicable following the receipt by the Company of such Listings, pricing and service descriptions. As reasonably necessary, the Company shall update the Specifications to reflect such Updates and shall provide a copy of same to MBE at least once per calendar quarter. If the Company develops additional Intellectual Property Rights that are made available without development charge to other customers of the Company, the Company will also make such Intellectual Property Rights available to MBE without development charge.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Update' as bug fixes and maintenance, whereas the changed text defines 'Update' as optional feature enhancements. The contradicted text, section 4(a), states that the company will make 'Updates' to the service at no charge. Since 'Update' now means optional feature enhancements, this contradicts the original meaning of mandatory bug fixes, creating an inconsistency and potential unenforceability regarding the company's obligation to provide free essential updates.
